rationale: A complete WMI persistence triad was established on host-01: an event filter (EV-001) triggers on logon of 'user-02', a CommandLineEventConsumer (A0) executes a base64-encoded PowerShell payload, and the filter-to-consumer binding (EV-002) ties them together — all created within milliseconds. DER-001 confirms the decoded payload disables SSL certificate validation and then downloads and executes arbitrary code via IEX from an internal IP (192.168.0.4:443/GoPro5/black/2018/_rp), a classic cradle pattern consistent with a C2 stager (e.g., Metasploit/Cobalt Strike). The consumer name 'WindowsParentalControlMigration' is a masquerading technique to blend in with legitimate Windows components. The two distinct user accounts involved (corp\user-02 creating the filter/consumer, user-01 creating the binding) may indicate lateral movement or privilege abuse.
